prompt: I'm finishing a master's in computer science and my research is on reinforcement learning for robotics — mostly sim-to-real transfer, and I have one workshop paper on domain randomization. I'm applying for PhDs starting in 2027. Can you do a deep dive on Professor Sergey Levine at UC Berkeley and tell me whether he'd be a good PhD fit for me? If he's a fit, I'd want to know what to mention if I email him.

assertions.text: Has YAML front-matter with fit_score field
Has a research agenda / open-problems section
Has a Sources section with links
Strong fit score (>=70) for on-target RL/robotics match
Reframes sim-to-real toward lab's real-world/foundation-model direction
